fields.description: Unique account identifier.

Primary owning entity. Immutable after creation. This is the entity
under which the account appears for 5300 reporting and BSA monitoring.

Ledger account type. Maps to 5300 account codes via the bookkeeping layer.
- `checking` — Share draft account (5300 code 025B)
- `savings` — Regular share account (5300 code 010)
- `money_market` — Money market share (5300 code 012)

Account lifecycle state.
- `open` — Active and operational.
- `frozen` — Temporarily suspended (no debits; credits may still post). Reversible.
- `closed` — Permanently closed (terminal). Balance must be zero.
Transitions: OPEN to/from FROZEN, FROZEN to CLOSED, OPEN to CLOSED.

Orthogonal lock applied independently of entity status.
When lock_type is not `none`, the entity is restricted from certain operations
even if status is `active`. Multiple lock types cannot stack — the most
restrictive active lock takes precedence at the application layer.

Orthogonal dormancy classification, tracked independently of account status.
Driven by state-specific inactivity periods (typically 12 months dormant,
3-5 years abandoned). Controls CD-07 (Dormancy Monitoring) and
CD-08 (Escheatment) evaluate this flag.
ISO 4217 currency code. Only USD supported.

Balance fields sourced from the Blnk Deploy ledger. These are read-only
computed values — all mutations happen through transaction endpoints.
Amounts are in the smallest currency unit (cents for USD).
Current available balance (cents). Credits minus debits, excluding inflight.
Lifetime credit total (cents).
Lifetime debit total (cents).

Net pending transaction amount (cents). Represents authorized but
unsettled transactions (e.g., card authorizations). Managed by Blnk's
QUEUED to INFLIGHT to APPLIED/VOID/REJECTED lifecycle.
Pending inbound credits (cents).
Pending outbound debits (cents).

Shadow bookkeeping layer fields for 5300 regulatory reporting and FBO
segregation. These are managed by Cassandra, not sourced from Blnk.

5300 Schedule A line item code for balance sheet classification.
Used by the 5300 Reporter consumer for aggregated reporting.

FBO sub-account classification for the bookkeeping layer.
- `customer_balance` — Holds attributed to a specific end-user entity.
- `commingled_cash` — Pooled operational funds (e.g., partner float).
- `settlement` — Funds in transit for Fed settlement.

Amount held by BSA/compliance controls (cents). Separate from Blnk
inflight holds — this represents funds frozen by the control engine
(e.g., BA-05 OFAC match, fraud investigation). Reduces effective
available balance as effective_available = balance - locked_amount.

All entities with ownership or authority over this account.
At minimum, includes the primary owner matching entity_id.
Joint ownership is modeled here, not at the entity level.
Corresponding ledger ID in Blnk Deploy. Opaque reference for reconciliation.

Summary of account numbers associated with this account.
Use the Account Numbers endpoints for full details and management.
Arbitrary key-value pairs for partner use.
Unique account number resource identifier.
The ledger account this number is associated with. Immutable.

12-digit account number allocated by the aggregator.
Format is PPP-SSSSSSSS-C where P is the 3-digit fintech prefix,
S is the 8-digit sequence, and C is the Luhn check digit.
Displayed with dashes for readability; transmitted as 12 contiguous
digits in ACH/wire files. Never reused.

ABA routing number. All account numbers within a credit union share the
same routing number (the credit union's Fed master account routing number).
Inbound payments are routed to the correct fintech instance by the
aggregator using the 3-digit account number prefix.

Account number lifecycle state.
- `active` — Routable. Inbound payments accepted, outbound allowed.
- `disabled` — Temporarily non-routable (reversible). Inbound payments rejected.
- `canceled` — Permanently deactivated (terminal). Number is never reused.
Transitions: ACTIVE to/from DISABLED, ACTIVE or DISABLED to CANCELED.

Entity ID for FBO attribution. Typically matches the account's primary
owner but can differ in BaaS scenarios where the fintech partner is the
account holder and end-users are tracked at the account number level.
Used by the bookkeeping layer for per-entity FBO segregation and
BSA monitoring at the account number level.

Human-readable label for this account number (e.g., Primary Checking,
Partner A Routing). For partner reference only, not used in payment files.
Unique ACH transfer identifier.
The ledger account being debited or credited.

The specific account number used for this transfer. Determines the
routing/account number pair that appears in the ACH file.

Direction of the ACH transfer from the perspective of this account.
- `credit` — Push money out (originate a payment to an external party).
- `debit` — Pull money in (originate a collection from an external party).
Transfer amount in cents. Must be positive.

ACH transfer lifecycle state.
- `pending_approval` — Created, awaiting control engine evaluation.
- `submitted` — Controls passed, ACH file submitted to Fed.
- `settled` — Funds have settled at the Fed.
- `returned` — Settled transfer was returned by receiving bank.
- `rejected` — Control engine rejected the transfer (never submitted).
- `canceled` — Canceled after submission but before settlement.
Transitions: PENDING_APPROVAL to SUBMITTED or REJECTED, SUBMITTED to SETTLED or CANCELED, SETTLED to RETURNED.

ACH processing window.
- `standard` — Next-day settlement (standard ACH).
- `same_day` — Same-day ACH (additional fee may apply, cutoff times enforced).
ABA routing number of the receiving institution.
Account number at the receiving institution.
Account type at the receiving institution.

Receiver name as it will appear in the ACH file.
Max 22 characters per NACHA format.

Company entry description in the ACH batch header.
Max 10 characters per NACHA format (e.g., "PAYROLL", "PAYMENT").

Originator company name in the ACH batch header.
Max 16 characters per NACHA format. Defaults to partner's registered name.

The date the transfer will settle at the Fed. Set by the system based
on the processing window and submission time. For same-day ACH, this is
today (if before cutoff) or next business day.

Estimated settlement timestamp. More precise than effective_date —
accounts for Fed processing windows, weekends, and holidays.
Actual settlement timestamp. Populated when status becomes `settled`.
Timestamp of the return. Populated when status becomes `returned`.

NACHA return reason code (e.g., R01 Insufficient Funds, R02 Account Closed,
R03 No Account, R04 Invalid Account Number). Only present when status is `returned`.
Human-readable return reason. Only present when status is `returned`.

Explanation of why the control engine rejected this transfer.
Only present when status is `rejected`.

Results of all control engine evaluations for this transfer.
Populated at creation time (when the control engine gates the transfer).
Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy.
Unique book transfer identifier.
The account being debited.
The account being credited.
Transfer amount in cents.

Book transfer lifecycle state.
- `pending` — Created, control engine evaluating.
- `completed` — Funds moved instantly (both sides settled).
- `rejected` — Control engine rejected the transfer.
Book transfers are instant and on-us, so there is no submitted/returned flow.
Free-text description of the transfer purpose.

Explanation of why the control engine rejected this transfer.
Only present when status is `rejected`.
Results of control engine evaluations.
Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy.
Unique wire transfer identifier.
The ledger account being debited (outbound) or credited (inbound).
The account number used for this wire.

Direction of the wire transfer.
- `outbound` — Originated by this instance, sending funds via Fedwire.
- `inbound` — Received from an external bank via Fedwire, routed by the
  aggregator to this instance based on account number prefix.
Transfer amount in cents.

Wire transfer lifecycle state.
- `pending_approval` — Created, awaiting control engine evaluation.
- `submitted` — Controls passed, Fedwire message submitted.
- `completed` — Wire completed (funds irrevocably transferred).
- `return_requested` — A return has been requested after completion.
- `returned` — Return request honored by receiving bank; funds returned.
- `rejected` — Control engine rejected the transfer (never submitted).
- `canceled` — Canceled after submission but before completion.
Transitions: PENDING_APPROVAL to SUBMITTED or REJECTED,
SUBMITTED to COMPLETED or CANCELED,
COMPLETED to RETURN_REQUESTED,
RETURN_REQUESTED to RETURNED or COMPLETED (request denied).
The receiving party and their bank details for an outbound wire.
Beneficiary name as it will appear on the Fedwire message.
ABA routing number of the beneficiary's bank.
Account number at the beneficiary's bank.

Name of the beneficiary's bank. Optional — if omitted, resolved
automatically from the routing number via the Fed directory.
Street address line 1.
Street address line 2 (apt, suite, unit, etc.).
Two-letter US state or territory code (ISO 3166-2:US).
US ZIP code (5-digit or ZIP+4).
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.

Originator information from an inbound Fedwire message. Read-only —
populated by the aggregator when routing an inbound wire.
Originator-to-beneficiary information (OBI) from the Fedwire message.

Originator-to-beneficiary information (OBI). Free-text description
of the wire purpose, included in the Fedwire message.

Input Message Accountability Data — the Fed's unique identifier for
this Fedwire message. Populated after submission. Used for tracing
and return requests.

Output Message Accountability Data — assigned by the receiving Fed
bank. Populated after the wire is acknowledged.
Timestamp when the Fedwire message was submitted.
Timestamp when the wire completed. Wires are typically same-day.

Details of the return request, if one has been filed.
Only present when status is `return_requested` or `returned`.

Explanation of why the control engine rejected this wire.
Only present when status is `rejected`.
Results of control engine evaluations.
Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy.
Unique verification identifier.
The entity being verified.

Whether this is identity verification for a person (KYC) or a
business (KYB). Trusts use KYB. Joint entities are verified through
their individual member persons (each gets a KYC verification).

The identity verification provider used for this check.
- `alloy`, `persona`, `socure`, `middesk` — Cassandra-managed providers.
  The system routes to the configured provider for the partner's instance.
- `partner_provided` — The partner ran their own KYC/KYB and is submitting
  an attestation. Trust level from partner configuration determines how
  much of the partner's result Cassandra accepts.

Trust level for partner-provided verifications (from partner configuration).
- `full` — Cassandra trusts the partner's identity verification result
  in full. OFAC screening still runs independently (compliance floor).
- `verify_watchlist_only` — Cassandra trusts the partner's identity data
  but re-runs all watchlist and sanctions checks (OFAC, SDN, PEP).

Status of this individual verification attempt.
- `pending` — Verification submitted to provider, awaiting result.
- `in_review` — Provider flagged for manual review (e.g., document required).
- `approved` — Verification passed. Entity kyc_status updated to `approved`.
- `denied` — Verification failed. Entity kyc_status updated to `denied`.
- `expired` — Verification result has expired per CDD refresh policy
  (typically 2 years for standard risk, 1 year for high risk).
  Entity kyc_status updated to `expired`.

Result of the mandatory OFAC screening. OFAC screening is a compliance
floor control (BA-05) and runs on every verification regardless of
provider or trust level. This cannot be disabled.

- `clear` — No matches found on SDN, consolidated sanctions, or PEP lists.
- `potential_match` — Possible match requiring compliance officer review.
  Entity is created with `lock_type: compliance` until resolved.
- `confirmed_match` — Confirmed SDN or sanctions list match. Entity blocked.

Names of lists that returned matches (e.g., "SDN", "Consolidated Sanctions",
"PEP", "314a"). Empty array when match_status is `clear`.

Confidence score of the strongest match (0-100). Only meaningful when
match_status is `potential_match`. Used by compliance officers to
prioritize review.

Normalized verification result from the provider. Cassandra translates
each provider's native response into this common structure.

The provider's native reference ID for this verification
(e.g., Alloy evaluation_token, Persona inquiry_id).

The provider's native status string, preserved for debugging.
Cassandra normalizes this into the verification's top-level `status`.
Whether the provider confirmed the identity matches the provided data.

Whether identity documents were verified (if required by risk tier).
Null if document verification was not required.

Provider's risk score, normalized to 0-100 range. Higher = riskier.
Used by CDD controls for risk tiering.

Risk signal tags from the provider (e.g., "address_mismatch",
"ssn_issued_prior_to_dob", "high_risk_jurisdiction"). Used by
the control engine for enhanced due diligence triggers.

The complete provider response, stored verbatim for audit purposes.
Only returned when the requesting token has compliance-scoped permissions.
Omitted in standard partner API responses.

Machine-readable reasons for denial (e.g., "identity_not_verified",
"document_mismatch", "high_risk_jurisdiction", "ofac_match").
Only present when status is `denied`.

When this verification result expires per CDD refresh policy.
After expiry, the entity's kyc_status transitions to `expired`
and a new verification is required. Expiry is risk-tier dependent
(2 years standard, 1 year high risk).

What triggered this verification.
- `initial` — First-time verification on entity creation.
- `cdd_refresh` — Periodic re-verification per CDD policy (CD-06).
- `information_change` — Re-verification triggered by a material
  change to the entity (e.g., name or address update).
- `manual` — Manually initiated by compliance officer or partner.
Unique card identifier.
The ledger account this card draws from / credits to.

The entity this card is issued to. Must be a person entity.
Immutable after creation.

- `virtual` — Instantly issued, PAN available immediately via secure details endpoint.
- `physical` — Requires fulfillment (mailing). PAN available after activation.

Card form factor for network classification.
- `debit` — Linked to a deposit account with overdraft rules.
- `prepaid` — Balance-limited, no overdraft.
Card network, determined by the BIN allocated to this instance.

Card lifecycle state, driven by the adapter interface
(createCard, activateCard, freezeCard, closeCard).
- `inactive` — Card created but not yet activated. Cannot be used for transactions.
- `active` — Card is live and can authorize transactions.
- `frozen` — Temporarily suspended (reversible). Authorizations are declined.
- `closed` — Permanently deactivated (terminal). Card number is retired.
Transitions: INACTIVE to ACTIVE, ACTIVE to/from FROZEN, ACTIVE or FROZEN to CLOSED.
Last four digits of the card number (PAN).
Four-digit expiration year.

Shipping details for physical cards. Only present when `card_type`
is `physical`. Null for virtual cards.

Optional per-card spending limits. These are enforced in addition to
account-level controls and the control engine's velocity limits.
Maximum daily spend in cents. Null means no per-card daily limit.
Maximum monthly spend in cents.
Maximum single transaction amount in cents.

Whitelist of allowed Merchant Category Codes. If populated, only
transactions at merchants with these MCCs are approved.
Empty array means all MCCs allowed.

Blacklist of blocked MCCs. Transactions at merchants with these
MCCs are declined. Evaluated after allowed_mcc_codes.

Card reference ID at the processor (Marqeta card_token or Lithic card_id).
Opaque — used internally for adapter operations.

Authorization lifecycle.
- `pending` — Authorization received, control engine evaluating (transient, sub-2-second).
- `approved` — Authorized. Blnk inflight hold created.
- `declined` — Control engine or balance check declined.
- `reversed` — Merchant reversed the authorization before settlement.
- `expired` — Authorization expired without settlement (processor-dependent, typically 7-30 days).
Authorization amount in cents.
Merchant details from the authorization request.
Merchant Category Code.
Card acceptor ID from the processor.
Machine-readable reason for declining an authorization.

Control engine results for this authorization. Includes balance check,
velocity, OFAC, fraud, and spend control evaluations.

Blnk inflight transaction ID (for approved authorizations).
Null for declined authorizations.
Processor's native authorization reference.

Settlement timestamp. Populated when the inflight transaction commits
in Blnk (processor sends settlement webhook). Null until settled.

Final settlement amount in cents. May differ from the authorization
amount (e.g., tip added, partial fulfillment).
Unique bookkeeping entry identifier.
The ledger account this entry pertains to.

The specific account number involved (if applicable).
Null for account-level entries (e.g., interest, fees).

The entity attributed for FBO segregation. Derived from the
account's primary owner or the account number's
informational_entity_id, depending on the transaction.

Type of bookkeeping entry.
- `debit` — Funds left the account (matches a Blnk debit transaction).
- `credit` — Funds entered the account (matches a Blnk credit transaction).
- `hold_placed` — Compliance hold placed by the control engine (increases locked_amount).
- `hold_released` — Compliance hold released (decreases locked_amount).
- `hold_seized` — Held funds permanently seized (e.g., forfeiture order).

The originating transaction type that produced this bookkeeping entry.
Maps 1:1 to the domain event that triggered the Blnk webhook.

ID of the originating resource (e.g., ach_5kRb3m for an ACH transfer,
auth_2nRb7m for a card authorization). Allows joining bookkeeping
entries back to their source transactions.
Entry amount in cents. Always positive — direction is conveyed by entry_type.

Account balance after this entry was applied (cents). Provides a
verifiable audit trail — each entry's running_balance should equal the
previous entry's running_balance plus or minus this entry's amount.

NCUA 5300 account code for this entry (e.g., 025B for share drafts,
010 for regular shares). Inherited from the account's bookkeeping tags
at the time the entry was created.

5300 Schedule A line item code for balance sheet classification.
Used by the 5300 Reporter for aggregated reporting.

FBO sub-account classification for the bookkeeping layer.
- `customer_balance` — Holds attributed to a specific end-user entity.
- `commingled_cash` — Pooled operational funds (e.g., partner float).
- `settlement` — Funds in transit for Fed settlement.

Corresponding Blnk Deploy transaction ID. Null for compliance hold
entries (which are Cassandra-native, not Blnk transactions).

System-generated description of the entry (e.g., "ACH credit from
John Doe via 271070801", "Compliance hold placed — BA-05 OFAC match").

The business date this entry is effective for regulatory reporting.
May differ from created_at for entries posted after business hours
or for backdated adjustments.
Unique event identifier.

Domain event type. Events are emitted on every state transition and
significant operation. Partners receive these via webhook delivery.
ID of the resource that changed (e.g., ent_abc123, acct_xyz789).
Type of the resource (e.g., entity, account, ach_transfer).

Snapshot of the resource at the time of the event. Contains the full
resource representation (same schema as the corresponding GET endpoint).
For update events, this is the state after the change.

For update events, the resource state before the change. Only includes
fields that changed. Null for creation events.

Delivery status for a specific webhook delivery attempt.
- `pending` — Queued for delivery (or retrying).
- `delivered` — Partner endpoint returned 2xx.
- `failed` — Delivery failed after all 12 retry attempts over 24 hours.
- `expired` — Event aged out of the delivery window without successful delivery.
Number of webhook delivery attempts made so far (max 12).
Timestamp of the most recent delivery attempt.
Timestamp of successful delivery. Null if not yet delivered.
Unique webhook endpoint identifier.

The HTTPS URL that receives webhook POST requests. Must use HTTPS
(HTTP rejected). Must return 2xx within 30 seconds to be considered
a successful delivery.

- `active` — Events are delivered to this endpoint.
- `disabled` — Delivery is paused. Events accumulate and can be
  retrieved via the backfill endpoint.

List of event type patterns this endpoint receives. Supports exact
types (e.g., "ach_transfer.settled") and wildcard prefixes
(e.g., "ach_transfer.*", "entity.*"). Use ["*"] to subscribe to
all events.

HMAC-SHA256 signing secret for verifying webhook payloads. Only
returned once at creation time. Use to verify the
`X-Cassandra-Signature` header on incoming webhooks.

Signature format: `t=<unix_timestamp>,v1=<hmac_sha256_hex>`

Verification:
1. Extract `t` and `v1` from the header.
2. Compute HMAC-SHA256 of `<t>.<raw_request_body>` using this secret.
3. Compare the computed signature to `v1`.
4. Reject if `t` is more than 5 minutes old (replay protection).
Rolling delivery statistics for this endpoint.

Consecutive failed deliveries. When this reaches a threshold,
the endpoint may be automatically disabled and the partner alerted.

Current FBO balance for this instance (cents). Sum of all settled
inbound minus all settled outbound for this instance's accounts.

Amount currently reserved for pending outbound payments (cents).
Held via the reserve saga pattern (Decision 28, step 4).

Effective available balance (cents). Computed as `balance - held_amount`.
This is the amount the Origination API checks against when approving
new outbound payments.

The sequence_id of the most recent event processed by the Payment Hub
consumer for this instance. Used for staleness detection.
The 12-digit account number this payment was routed to.

- `pending` — Aggregator has the payment, not yet pushed to instance.
- `delivered` — Pushed to instance via webhook.
- `acknowledged` — Instance confirmed receipt.
Originator reference or trace number.
When the aggregator received this payment from the Fed.
The event that triggered this alert.

Type of BSA alert generated by the BSA Approver (real-time) or
BSA Reporter (scheduled).

Hashed entity identifier (SSN/EIN hash). Used for per-entity
lookups without exposing PII in the alert table.

The fintech instance where the triggering event originated.
For cross-fintech alerts, this is the instance of the most
recent triggering event.

BSA alert lifecycle.
- `open` — New alert, not yet reviewed.
- `under_review` — Analyst is investigating.
- `escalated` — Elevated to SAR candidate or compliance officer.
- `cleared` — Reviewed and determined to be non-suspicious.
- `block` — Entity is blocked from payment origination. The Origination
  API checks for `block` status at step 2 of the origination flow.

Whether the BSA Reporter should run a deep historical analysis
on this alert. Set by the BSA Approver when it detects patterns
that need 90-day lookback.

Alert-specific details. Structure varies by alert_type.
Examples: transaction amounts, pattern description, matched
entity information for cross-fintech hits.

endpoints.summary: List account numbers for an account
Create a batch of ACH transfers
List bookkeeping entries for an account
Simulate: Receive an inbound ACH credit
Simulate: Settle an outbound ACH transfer
Simulate: Return a settled ACH transfer
Get FBO position for an instance
Get available balance for an instance
Confirm a prepared wire payment (phase 2 of 2)
List inbound payments for an instance
